rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY MURRAY ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 28, 2026, line 1. RESPONDENTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO HUMAN SERVICES AGENCY, TRENT RHORER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Hearing required. The court's tentative appears below. Respondents City and County of San Francisco, Board of Supervisors of the City and County of San Francisco, San Francisco Human Services Agency (HSA), and Trent Rhorer's motion for judgment on the pleadings regarding Petitioners' Second Amended Petition (SAP) is denied as to causes of action one, two, four, five, six, eight, nine, ten, and thirteen. The motion is granted with leave to amend as to causes of action seven, eleven, and twelve. The motion is granted without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JAIMIE DAVIS VS. BEUGELMANS, LLP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 28, 2026, line 2. PLAINTIFF JAIMIE DAVIS' Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award. Moving party Jaimie Davis has filed a petition to vacate an arbitration award. The arbitration award was previously confirmed by this court and judgment was entered. Davis has appealed the judgment. The court therefore lacks jurisdiction to revisit the judgment; the perfecting of an appeal from an order or judgment divests this court of jurisdiction over matters embracing or affecting the appeal. Davis appears to be making a distinction between Beugelmans and the other parties to the arbitration. Beugelmans is a party to the judgment, which has been appealed, and therefore the court lacks jurisdiction over claims related to the Beugelmans judgment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JAIMIE DAVIS VS. BEUGELMANS, LLP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 28, 2026, line 2. PLAINTIFF JAIMIE DAVIS' Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Costs. Moving party Jaimie Davis seeks an order striking $639 in costs sought by Qian & Nemecek LLP and David P. Nemecek (collectively "Nemecek"). The motion is denied. Davis moves on the ground that the arbitrator's determination of costs is conclusive and final, and the court may not add any costs the arbitrator has not awarded. Here, Nemecek's cost bill seeks litigation costs rather than costs incurred in arbitration. (See Cost Bill & Nemecek Decl., para. 6.) "A court must award costs in a judicial proceeding to confirm, correct or vacate an arbitration award." (Corona v. Amherst Partners (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 701, 707 [citing Code Civ. Proc. 1293.2; Carole Ring & Associates v. Nicastro (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 253].) The costs are therefore proper. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. OLAOLUWA J MAKANJUOLA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacatedefault Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 28, 2026, line 4. DEFENDANT OLAOLUWA MAKANJUOLA'S Motion To Vacate Default Judgment. Judgment debtor Olaoluwa J. Makanjuola's unopposed motion to vacate default judgment is granted. Makanjuola provides a declaration that he never received actual notice of this lawsuit until the judgment creditor sought to garnish his wages. Judgment creditor has filed no opposition. rulings.Case Title: MARK BALABANIAN ET AL VS. KIERAN O' CARROLL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 28, 2026, line 7. DEFENDANT KIERAN CARROLL'S Motion Of For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint. Defendant Kieran O'Carroll dba Kieran O'Carroll Construction's motion for leave to amend O'Carroll's cross complaint to add a cause of action for breach of contract is granted. Defendant's added breach cause of action is a compulsory cross-complaint because it is alleged against the plaintiff and arises out of the same transactions or events that give rise to the complaint; if it is not pled as a cross-complaint, it is forfeit. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 426.30.) "A policy of liberal construction of [Code of Civil Procedure] section 426.50 to avoid forfeiture of causes of action is imposed on the trial court. A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99.) Plaintiffs would have the court find bad faith based on mere delay, but inadvertence is not bad faith, and the court does not have a basis on this record to find bad faith. Moreover, the court sees not prejudice. Trial has been set for November 2026, and there is ample time for discovery. Plaintiffs further contend that the cross-complaint is futile because the statute of limitations bars the breach of contract cause of action. That issue may be raised through demurrer or summary judgment motion. The court grants plaintiffs' request for judicial notice. O'Carroll shall file the amended pleading submitted with its motion by February 9, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM L. VEEN, P.C., FKA THE VEEN FIRM, P.C., A VS. ELINOR LEARY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 28, 2026, line 11. DEFENDANT ELINOR LEARY'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Elinor Leary's demurrer to plaintiff William L. Veen, P.C.'s ("Veen") first amended complaint is overruled. The amended complaint alleges that the the $200,000 bonus advance loan was "specifically excluded from the True Up process" referenced in the Asset Purchase Agreement. (FAC para. 23.) The court must accept plaintiff's well-pleaded facts as true on demurrer. Leary contends that this fact is contradicted by the Asset Purchase Agreement, but accepting the contention would require the court to draw inferences against the pleading party on demurrer. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
